name: playground-snapshot-roundtrip
description: Snapshot round-trip — load the Tree scenario, save a labeled snapshot, switch to Random (so the active state changes), then restore the snapshot. The final screenshot should match the Tree state (active pill = Tree, viewport shows tree topology). Validates that PlaygroundSaveSnapshot + PlaygroundRestoreSnapshot persist graph + module config + camera correctly.

setup.vlmDescription: Debug Playground overlay with the 'Tree' pill highlighted (restored from snapshot). The viewport shows a tree-shaped graph of ~121 nodes. Crucially, the snapshot strip at the bottom of the overlay should now show at least one saved snapshot row labeled 'tree-baseline' with a recent timestamp ('Ns ago' or similar). The fact that the active scenario is Tree (not Random) demonstrates the restore overrode the intermediate Random switch.
commands: (async () => { const snaps = window.__canaryPlaygroundListSnapshots(); const tree = snaps.find(s => s.label === 'tree-baseline'); if (tree) window.__canaryPlaygroundRestoreSnapshot(tree.id); return tree ? tree.id : null; })()
